params.name: configKey
params.desc: REST client configuration key identifying the DocuWare connection from rest-clients.yaml

params.name: documentId
params.desc: The unique identifier of the document to move

params.name: sourceFileCabinetId
params.desc: The unique identifier of the source file cabinet containing the document

params.name: targetFileCabinetId
params.desc: The unique identifier of the target file cabinet to move the document into

params.name: result
params.desc: Result object containing the document query response after the transfer operation

name: **Description
This request transfers one or more documents from a Document Tray/File Cabinet to another Document Tray/File Cabinet.
**Integration
moveDocument(String,String,String,String)
- Moves a document from one file cabinet to another in DocuWare.
- Calls REST endpoint /FileCabinets/{FileCabinetId}/Task/Transfer via the move file RestClientCall in TransferService.p.json.
- Prepares the request body by converting source cabinet and document IDs into JSON with:
+ DocuWareTransferService.get().moveDocumentRequestBody(in.sourceFileCabinetId, in.documentId)
- Sends the resulting raw JSON as FileCabinetIdDocumentsBody in the POST body.
- Returns the DocuWare DocumentsQueryResult object.
Parameters:
- configKey (String): REST client configuration key to identify the DocuWare server connection from rest-clients.yaml
- sourceFileCabinetId (String): The unique identifier of the source file cabinet containing the document
- documentId (String): The unique identifier of the document to move
- targetFileCabinetId (String): The unique identifier of the target file cabinet to move the document into
- Default / Return: Returns a DocuWare DocumentsQueryResult object containing the document query response after the transfer operation.
**Prepare request body
Prepare data request body to call Docuware APIs
**move file
Call REST Client APIs from Docuware.
**How to integrate
- Add Call: Add a Call element to your process that invokes the TransferService sub-process using signature moveDocument(String,String,String,String).
- Prepare data: Populate the parameters before calling:
+ configKey: REST client configuration key matching the entry in config/rest-clients.yaml
+ documentId: ID of the document to move
+ sourceFileCabinetId: ID of the source file cabinet containing the document
+ targetFileCabinetId: ID of the target file cabinet to move the document into
- Build request body: TransferService.p.json prepares the raw JSON payload in the script step:
+ DocuWareTransferService.get().moveDocumentRequestBody(in.sourceFileCabinetId, in.documentId)
+ This produces in.requestJson
+ The move file RestClientCall sends that raw JSON as FileCabinetIdDocumentsBody in the POST body
- Call REST API: The move file RestClientCall sends the transfer request to:
+ POST /FileCabinets/{FileCabinetId}/Task/Transfer
- Return object: The call returns a com.docuware.dev.schema._public.services.platform.DocumentsQueryResult
+ map the process result to result.result
+ use the returned object to confirm the transfer response
- Post-success behavior: After success, the document is transferred into the target file cabinet
+ inspect result to confirm the transfer outcome and any returned document query details
- Example (conceptual):
+ Set variables:
configKey = "02d1eec1-32e9-4316-afc3-793448486203"
sourceFileCabinetId = "FC-123"
documentId = "DOC-456"
targetFileCabinetId = "FC-789"
+ Call the TransferService sub-process with the matching signature
+ After the Call returns, read result for confirmation
- Notes & tips:
+ configKey must match the RestClients entry so URL and credentials are correct
+ sourceFileCabinetId and targetFileCabinetId must refer to valid DocuWare file cabinets
+ This operation does not upload a file; it transfers an existing document between cabinets
+ Use the returned DocumentsQueryResult to verify the transfer and check the moved document metadata

Summary: Add a Call to TransferService with moveDocument(String,String,String,String), set configKey, documentId, sourceFileCabinetId, and targetFileCabinetId, let the process build the transfer JSON, call POST /FileCabinets/{FileCabinetId}/Task/Transfer, and verify the returned DocumentsQueryResult.
